Strength.progression_rules: Linear loading progression. Add 2.5-5kg to lower body compounds weekly if target reps are achieved with perfect technique.

Power.progression_rules: Velocity-based progression. Monitor bar speed. Increase load only if mean velocity remains above 0.90 m/s. Deload if velocity drops below 0.75 m/s.

Acceleration.progression_rules: Focus on horizontal force production. Progression is volume-based (accumulate sprints up to 100m total distance) followed by resisted sprint loading (10% bodyweight sled).

Speed.progression_rules: Neurological recovery rule. Full rest (1 min per 10m run) is mandatory. Increase total sprints by 1 weekly if top speed does not drop.

Rotational Power.progression_rules: Velocity transfer. Progress from slow, heavy anti-rotation movements (Pallof press) to explosive, light throwing volume (release speed focus).

Strength.progression_rules: Double progression. Increase reps from 6 to 8 over weeks. Once 8 reps are hit for all sets, increase load by 5% and reset reps to 6.

Power.progression_rules: Qualitative movement speed. Increase jump distance/height weekly while maintaining high movement quality and landing stability.

Acceleration.progression_rules: Lateral and multi-directional starts. Focus on reactive agility cues. Progression shifts from planned sprints to randomized auditory/visual reaction starts.

Speed.progression_rules: Max velocity maintenance. Progression is based on maintaining sprint times. Add 5m to sprint length when times are stable within 2% margin.

Rotational Power.progression_rules: Torque separation progression. Focus on pelvis-to-thorax separation angle. Progress by increasing rotational throwing speed or adding resisted cable loads.

Strength.progression_rules: Linear load progression. Increase load by 2-5% when all reps in the target range are achieved.

Power.progression_rules: Contrast progression. Alternate heavy strength sets with explosive power sets (e.g. Back Squat at 85% 1RM followed by SL Bounds) to exploit post-activation potentiation (PAP).

Acceleration.progression_rules: Sprint volume and setup progression. Progress from standing starts to deep athletic/crouch starts (carrying bat). Increase volume from 5 sprints of 15m to 8 sprints of 15m over a block.

Speed.progression_rules: Deceleration and change of direction (COD) integration. Progress from linear sprint to sprint-decelerate-turn. Monitor turning transition times.

Rotational Power.progression_rules: Power progression. Increase the mass of the medicine ball by 1kg if throw velocity is maintained, or increase cable resistance by 5%.

Strength.progression_rules: Volume accumulation. Focus on time-under-tension (TUT) and isometric hold durations. Increase hold time by 5s weekly before increasing load.

Power.progression_rules: Stretch-Shortening Cycle (SSC) progression. Focus on minimizing ground contact time (GCT < 150ms). Increase drop height by 5cm weekly up to 45cm limit.

Acceleration.progression_rules: Agility and lateral start rules. Progress by adding obstacles or bands to resisted lateral shuffles. Reduce ground response times.

Speed.progression_rules: Vertical to horizontal transition. Start from ground squat/sitting and transition to full sprint speed. Progress weekly by adding complex reactive start signals.

Rotational Power.progression_rules: Positional constraint progression. Progress from standing to kneeling, then to split-squat stance throws to increase core demand.

Strength.progression_rules: Linear loading. Add 2.5-5kg to main compound lifts every 2 weeks.

Power.progression_rules: Linear power progression. Increase load by 2.5% weekly if explosive power velocity targets are met.

Acceleration.progression_rules: Linear volume progression. Increase total acceleration distance from 60m to 100m over the 4-week block.

Speed.progression_rules: Rest-interval reduction or volume progression. Maintain max output with full neurological recovery (3-4 mins rest between repetitions).

Rotational Power.progression_rules: Volume and complexity progression. Increase reps from 6 to 8, then progress to dynamic stepping starts before the rotational release.
